person.name: Ruixiang Xue
person.title: Ph.D. Student, Nanjing University
person.summary: Spatial intelligence: intelligent point cloud compression, 3D Gaussian splatting compression, implicit neural representations, driving world models, street-scene novel-view synthesis, and AI-assisted photography.

education.degree: Ph.D. Student, Information and Communication Engineering
details: NJU Vision Lab, advised by Prof. Zhan Ma
Research fields: intelligent point cloud compression and 3D Gaussian splatting compression
Excellent result in doctoral qualification assessment

experience.summary: Working on driving world models, dynamic street-scene novel-view synthesis, and restoration of world-model or 3D scene generation artifacts.
bullets: Studied and analyzed driving world model and street-view synthesis methods including Vista, StreetCrafter, SymDrive, FreeFix, and DriveFix.
Built evaluation and deployment workflows around StreetCrafter and UniSplat-style baselines for dynamic street-scene generation experiments.
Explored restoration training pairs and confidence or outlier filtering strategies for novel-view artifacts in driving scenes.

experience.summary: Worked on intelligent point cloud compression research and MPEG AI-PCC standardization.
bullets: Developed and evaluated intelligent point cloud compression algorithms around MPEG AI-PCC standardization.
Implemented point cloud codec software and evaluated performance across standard test sequences.
Participated in multiple MPEG meetings, submitted 8 standardization proposals, and filed 5 invention patents.

projects.name: Intelligent Point Cloud Compression
projects.summary: Research and development of deep learning based point cloud compression methods for MPEG AI-PCC.
bullets: Designed Transformer models for point cloud representation and efficient spatial feature extraction.
Used density changes during point cloud downsampling to guide reconstruction mode selection.
Achieved 39% geometry compression gain and 34% attribute compression gain over the baseline under public test conditions.

projects.name: 3D Gaussian Splatting Coding
projects.summary: Compact and progressive compression for 3D Gaussian splatting scenes.
bullets: Investigated compactification, compression, and reconstruction methods for 3D Gaussian splatting.
Proposed RecastGS to reorganize pretrained 3DGS into a region-aware layered hierarchy with prompt-driven object extraction and progressive distillation.
Proposed LayeredCGS, a feed-forward layered 3DGS compressor with cross-layer context modeling and truncatable bitstreams.
Achieved 35% BD-Rate gain over the feed-forward FCGS baseline, with up to 2 dB higher ROI PSNR under comparable bitrates.
The resulting paper, 3D Gaussian Splatting Compression with Object Scalability, has been accepted by ECCV 2026 and is not yet on arXiv.

projects.name: Driving World Model and Street-Scene Restoration
projects.summary: Research exploration on dynamic street-scene generation, novel-view artifacts, and restoration pipelines for driving simulation.
bullets: Surveyed Vista-style driving world models and StreetCrafter-style controllable street-view video diffusion methods.
Compared restoration and refinement directions including Difix3D+, SymDrive, FreeFix, and DriveFix to identify gaps in artifact distribution, temporal consistency, and input requirements.
Explored evaluation matrices for StreetCrafter and UniSplat baselines, including generated views, real references, and quantitative summaries.
Focused on constructing degraded-clean pairs closer to inference-time novel-view artifacts and filtering unreliable pseudo supervision.

projects.name: Intelligent Camera Assistant
projects.summary: A user-friendly camera assistant prototype for composition guidance and shooting suggestions.
bullets: Surveyed photo composition, feed-forward 3DGS reconstruction, and large-model based aesthetic quality assessment methods.
Built an AI coding demo with image analysis, composition advice, composition preview generation, and step-by-step shooting guidance.

publications.title: 3D Gaussian Splatting Compression with Object Scalability
publications.authors: Ruixiang Xue et al. (authors to be finalized after public release)
publications.abstract: We introduce a framework towards scalable, finer-grained object-level 3D Gaussian Splatting compression. RecastGS reorganizes pretrained 3DGS into a region-aware layered hierarchy for object-level and ROI-adaptive quality control. LayeredCGS then compresses position with a lightweight point cloud codec and attributes with cross-layer context modeling, producing truncatable bitstreams for low-latency preview and progressive refinement.
bullets: Introduced RecastGS, a post-training method that reorganizes pretrained 3DGS into a region-aware layered hierarchy for object-level and ROI-adaptive quality control.
Introduced LayeredCGS, a feed-forward layered 3DGS compressor that models cross-layer dependencies and generates truncatable bitstreams for fast preview and progressive refinement.
Achieved 35% BD-Rate gain over FCGS and up to 2 dB higher ROI PSNR than uniform quality allocation at comparable bitrates.

publications.title: A Versatile Point Cloud Compressor Using Universal Multiscale Conditional Coding-Part I Geometry
publications.authors: Jianqiang Wang, Ruixiang Xue, Jiaxin Li, Dandan Ding, Yi Lin, Zhan Ma
publications.venue_full: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
publications.venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
publications.notes: SCI Q1, CCF-A, impact factor 18.6, co-first author
publications.abstract: Proposed a universal multiscale conditional point cloud geometry coding framework that supports lossy/lossless, static/dynamic, and diverse point cloud data, outperforming MPEG G-PCC, V-PCC, and learning-based baselines with strong compression efficiency.
bullets: Proposed a universal multiscale conditional point cloud geometry coding framework supporting lossy/lossless, static/dynamic, and diverse point cloud data.
Outperformed MPEG G-PCC, V-PCC, and learning-based methods with second-level codec complexity; improved lossless compression by 30% and lossy compression by 92% overall.

publications.title: NeRI: Implicit Neural Representation of LiDAR Point Cloud Using Range Image Sequence
publications.authors: Ruixiang Xue, Jiaxin Li, Tong Chen, Dandan Ding, Xun Cao, Zhan Ma
publications.venue_full: IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech, and Signal Processing
publications.venue: IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech, and Signal Processing 2024
publications.abstract: Proposed the first implicit neural representation method for LiDAR point cloud compression by converting 3D point clouds into range image sequences and coding them with spatiotemporal conditional neural networks, reaching real-time decoding and strong low-bitrate gains.
bullets: Proposed the first implicit neural representation method for LiDAR point cloud compression by converting 3D point clouds into 2D range images and coding them with spatiotemporal conditional neural networks.
Reached 376 FPS real-time decoding and achieved 91% overall gain at low bitrates over MPEG G-PCC, HEVC, and other learning-based point cloud compression methods.

publications.title: A Unified Point Cloud Compressor Using Universal Multiscale Conditional Coding-Part II Attribute
publications.venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
publications.notes: SCI Q1, CCF-A, impact factor 18.6, second author

publications.title: GRNet: Geometry Restoration for G-PCC Compressed Point Clouds Using Auxiliary Density Signaling
publications.venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
publications.notes: SCI Q1, CCF-A, impact factor 6.5, second author

items: Deep learning based point cloud coding
